message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implementing dry_run option for Image.save(); primarily touches Image.save() method and its format-specific save handlers. Need to know your feature/files to avoid overlap.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implementing backup=True/backup_suffix param on Image.save() in Image.py, with tests in Tests/test_image.py

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both modify Image.save() so file-level disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing shared ownership with coordination instead: (1) I will add a new 'dry_run' keyword-only-style param, checked at the very end of save() right before the actual encoder.write/fp writing step - I won't touch the top of save() (open fp, format resolution) or add unrelated params. (2) Please keep your backup/backup_suffix logic near the top of save() (before file open/write) and avoid touching the encoder-call/dry_run area at the bottom. (3) In test_image.py we'll each add new standalone test functions (not edit each other's), so no line conflicts. Please confirm ACCEPT if this works for you.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Both our features (backup=True/backup_suffix vs dry_run) extend Image.save() signature, so true file disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: we each work in our own workspace copy independently. To keep patches mergeable/non-conflicting: (1) add new keyword params via kwargs.pop() near the top of save() before the filename/fp resolution logic, (2) do not rename/remove existing params or reorder unrelated code, (3) keep new logic in small self-contained blocks/helper methods rather than interleaving with existing lines, (4) in test_image.py add new test functions/classes rather than editing existing ones. This should let both diffs apply cleanly independent of merge order. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I'll keep dry_run logic self-contained: pop dry_run from kwargs near top of save() alongside existing param handling, guard the actual fp-write/rename step at the end with 'if not dry_run:'. Won't touch backup-related code. New test function(s) only in test_image.py. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed: I will add backup/backup_suffix handling near the top of save() (before fp/filename resolution and before any write occurs), as a self-contained block, and will not touch the encoder-call/dry_run area at the bottom. In test_image.py I'll add new standalone test functions only. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished dry_run implementation. Changes: added ':param dry_run:' docstring line, 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after the existing 'save_all = params.pop(...)' line, and a new 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO(); open_fp = False' branch before the existing 'elif open_fp:' block (was 'if open_fp:'). Tests added as new standalone functions test_dry_run_save and test_dry_run_save_error in TestImage class, right before test_dump. Didn't touch anything above the save_all/dry_run pop lines or your backup-related area. All 194 existing tests + 2 new tests pass.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implemented backup/backup_suffix params: popped near top of save() alongside save_all, backup logic placed right before builtins.open() (before file-open/write), only in the open_fp branch. Docs added to save() docstring. 4 new standalone tests added in TestImage. Full test_image.py suite passes (196 passed, 1 skipped). Did not touch the bottom of save() near the encoder-call/dry_run area. Good luck!
